_comment: Ocelot route table. Each route maps an upstream path (what the Angular frontend calls) to a downstream path (the actual microservice endpoint). The gateway runs on https://localhost:7217 and all services run on plain HTTP internally.

Routes._comment: Auth service — handles register, login, OTP, password reset, and Google OAuth. All auth traffic goes to Auth.API on port 5259.
Booking service — handles bookings with path segments (e.g. /booking/my, /booking/{id}/cancel). Must be defined before the base /booking route so Ocelot matches the more specific pattern first.
Booking service — base route for POST /booking (create) and GET /booking (list all). Needs its own entry because /booking with no trailing segment doesn't match the {everything} wildcard above.
Hotel service — base route for GET /hotels (list all) and POST /hotels (create). Same reason as /booking above — needs its own entry for the no-segment case.

Hotel service — /hotels/my is a named route that returns only the calling manager's hotels. It must be defined BEFORE the generic /hotels/{everything} wildcard, otherwise Ocelot would match 'my' as the {everything} segment and route it incorrectly.
Hotel service — wildcard route for all other hotel paths: /hotels/{id}, /hotels/city/{city}, etc. This must come AFTER the more specific /hotels/my route above.
Reviews — base route for POST /reviews (submit a review) and the can-review check. Routes to Hotel.API since reviews are stored there alongside hotel data.

GlobalConfiguration._comment: BaseUrl is the public-facing URL of this gateway. Ocelot uses this when generating redirect URLs and Location headers.
